commit.message:
feat: Wave 2 — OIDC Core Compliance & Refresh Token Security (#65)

* docs: add implementation plan for LLM-driven security scanning

17-task plan covering kustomize overlays, deploy scripts, 4 MVP
scanners (flow-tester, timing-analyzer, error-leakage, token-entropy),
and main orchestration entrypoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: enable refresh_token grant with client auth and scope downscoping

- Add get_token_by_refresh_token to Storage trait + all impls
  (SqlxStorage, MongoStorage, ObservedStorage)
- Add ValidateRefreshToken actor message to TokenActor
- Implement handle_refresh_token_grant with client auth, scope
  downscoping, and token reissuance
- Wire refresh_token grant type in token dispatcher
- Flip include_refresh to true in auth code grant
- Update discovery document with refresh_token grant type
- Add SQLite index on tokens.refresh_token
- Add positive + negative integration tests

* feat: add nonce validation for OIDC Core conformance

- Add nonce field to AuthorizationCode model
- Thread nonce through CreateAuthorizationCode actor message
- Parse nonce from /oauth/authorize query params
- Store nonce in SQLite (bootstrap DDL) and Postgres (V9 migration)
- Echo nonce verbatim in id_token JWT claims
- Advertise nonce in discovery claims_supported
- Integration test: id_token_echoes_nonce_from_authorize_request

OIDC Core §3.1.2.1 compliance

* feat(oidc): add c_hash claim to ID token (OIDC Core §3.3.2.11)

- Add `c_hash: Option<String>` field to `IdTokenClaims` (with serde skip)
- Compute c_hash in `oauth.rs` token handler: left half of SHA-256 of
  the authorization code string, base64url-encoded (no padding)
- Same formula as `at_hash` but applied to the authorization code
- Add `c_hash` and `at_hash` to `claims_supported` in discovery endpoint
- Integration test: extract code from redirect, independently compute
  expected c_hash, assert exact match in id_token JWT payload

* feat: add refresh token rotation with replay detection per OAuth 2.0 Security BCP §4.13.2

- Add token_family UUID column to tokens table for grant lineage tracking
- Auth code exchange mints tokens with a new token_family UUID
- Refresh token grant propagates the family to the new token pair
- Replay detection: presenting a revoked refresh token revokes the entire
  token family, invalidating all tokens from that authorization grant
- ObservedStorage now delegates revoke_token_family to the inner store
  (was silently using the trait's default no-op)
- get_token_by_refresh_token no longer filters by revoked=0, allowing
  the replay detection handler to inspect revoked tokens
- Integration test validates the full RT1→RT2→replay→family-revoke flow

* fix: address all review feedback - grant type check, refresh token expiry, replay detection, mongo filter, migration rename

commit.message:
fix(runtime): wire wasm actor arenas into spawn lifecycle (#929)

* feat(runtime): wire real arena allocation into WASM spawn path

- In wasm32 spawn_actor_internal, replace arena: ptr::null_mut() with
  crate::arena::hew_arena_new().cast(), giving every spawned WASM actor
  a real bump-allocator arena from birth.

- Add OOM guard: if hew_arena_new() returns null, free state/init_state
  and mailbox then propagate null — mirrors the native spawn path.

- In free_actor_resources_wasm, add null-checked arena free via
  crate::arena::hew_arena_free_all, completing the lifecycle symmetry
  with the native free_actor_resources path.

- Promote free_actor_resources_wasm to pub(crate) so the
  scheduler_wasm test module can call it directly.

- Update the HewActor.arena doc-comment for wasm32 to reflect the
  field is now a live arena pointer, not a stub null.

- Add scheduler_wasm::tests::spawn_path_arena_is_installed_during_
  dispatch_and_freed_on_teardown: proves (a) a spawn-path actor carries
  a non-null arena, (b) activate_actor_wasm installs it as the current
  arena during dispatch and restores null afterwards, and (c)
  free_actor_resources_wasm teardown completes without crash.

Validation:
  cargo test -p hew-runtime --lib              1045 pass / 0 fail
  cargo build -p hew-runtime --target wasm32-wasip1     --no-default-features --release            clean build

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(runtime): fix stale comments after wasm arena wiring

Two comments contradicted the arena lifecycle changes landed in the
previous commit:

1. actor.rs free_actor_resources (wasm32 wrapper): doc said 'no arena
   cleanup' — replaced with 'delegates to free_actor_resources_wasm'.

2. scheduler_wasm.rs activate_actor_wasm: comment claimed the null-arena
   fast-path was intentional because all WASM actors carry a null arena
   — replaced with wording that reflects the new behaviour: WASM actors
   now carry a real arena allocated at spawn time, and both arena_install
   and arena_reset perform live work; null is still handled safely for
   manually constructed test actors.

No logic changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(runtime): satisfy lint gate for wasm arena test

Apply incremental performance optimizations

1. Cache UDP offset per reader: compute Ethernet→IP→UDP payload offset
   once per PCAP file (first packet), reuse for all 52M subsequent packets.
   Replaces per-packet UdpExtractor.ExtractUdpPayload call with a simple
   Memory.Slice(cachedOffset).

2. Span-based hot path: MessageDispatcher.Dispatch accepts pre-computed
   ReadOnlySpan<byte>, eliminating redundant .Data.Span virtual calls
   through MemoryManager in the ChannelHandler→Dispatch pipeline.

3. Pre-size BookManager dictionary: new EnsureCapacity() called after
   instrument definitions complete (18,380 instruments), avoids rehashing
   during the hot path.

4. Eliminate redundant dictionary lookup: BookSide.AddOrUpdate now returns
   bool (isUpdate), removing the separate ContainsKey call in
   BookManager.HandleOrder (saves 15.7M dictionary lookups).

Performance: warm cache 20.4s → 19.6s, sys time 2.8s → 1.7s (-39%)

fix: normalize get_installed_openclaw_version to extract semver

openclaw --version now outputs 'OpenClaw 2026.4.9 (0512059)' in newer
versions, but get_latest_openclaw_version() returns just '2026.4.9' from
the npm registry. The string comparison always differed, causing the
config-changed hook to endlessly reinstall openclaw even when already at
the latest version, leaving the hook in an error state.

Fix by piping the openclaw --version output through grep -oE to extract
only the semver portion, making both sides of the comparison consistent.

* feat(webgl): add pure WebGL2 distortion grid background

Pure WebGL2 implementation — no Three.js, only raw API calls and GLSL 300 ES
shaders. Renders thin black lines on a white background with:

- Stretched grid: 30×30 lines with power-curve spacing (1.3 exponent) —
  lines denser at edges, wider at center for perspective effect
- Mouse distortion: vertices displace away from cursor with aspect-corrected
  gaussian falloff (radius 0.35, strength 0.12), smoothly lerped each frame
- Breathing animation: subtle sine oscillation (0.3% amplitude) keeps the
  grid alive without mouse interaction
- Each line subdivided into 80 segments for smooth vertex displacement

- src/lib/webgl/gridScene.ts: shaders, mesh generation, render loop,
  resize handling, visibility pause, full cleanup

commit.message:
[AgentServer] Builder lifecycle ownership: each builder owns its full event lifecycle (#58094)

* Refactor builder lifecycle: each builder owns its full event lifecycle

Each content/refusal builder now owns its complete lifecycle from
EmitAdded() through EmitDone(), matching the Python SDK pattern where
parent builders auto-track children instead of manually tracking
completed content.

Changes:
- TextContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitTextDone(string?) for
  output_text.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done; tracks
  deltas and annotations internally; adds Annotations property
- RefusalContentBuilder: EmitDone(string) → EmitRefusalDone(string)
  for refusal.done; new EmitDone() for content_part.done
- OutputItemMessageBuilder: remove EmitContentDone() overloads;
  AddTextContent/AddRefusalContent auto-track children; EmitDone()
  builds content from tracked children
- OutputItemReasoningItemBuilder: remove EmitSummaryPartDone();
  AddSummaryPart auto-tracks children; EmitDone() builds from
  tracked children
- Update TextResponse, all tests (29 files), samples, and docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename stale test methods to match new builder API names

Replace BufferedStream+ArrayPool with zero-copy mmap PCAP reader

MmapPcapReader uses MemoryMappedFile for zero-copy I/O:
- Packet data points directly into mmap'd pages (no allocations, no copies)
- madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) enables aggressive kernel read-ahead
- Segmented Memory<byte> views handle files >2GB (1GB segments)
- UnmanagedMemoryManager bridges unsafe pointers to Memory<byte>

TimestampMergedReplayer simplified:
- Removed ArrayPool lifecycle tracking (_previousRented, ReturnRented)
- Mmap data is valid for replayer lifetime — no buffer management needed
- FeedHandler.EnqueueCopy no longer needs deep copy

Performance (52M packets, 15.7M orders, 3,639 books):
- Before: ~29s (BufferedStream + ArrayPool)
- After:  ~25s cold cache, ~20s warm cache (2.6M pkts/s)

Fix print engine offset for non-zero viewBox origin elements

Forge-exported elements have a tight viewBox with non-zero x/y origin
(e.g. '85 196 930 494'). The print engine was computing the inner
transform offset as -w/2, -h/2 without subtracting the origin, causing
elements to shift down/right in print vs workspace.

Fix: offX = -vb[0] - vb[2]/2, offY = -vb[1] - vb[3]/2

Native elements (0 0 w h origin) are unaffected.

commit.message:
Add per-channel effect routing and channel selection UI

Master effects can now be routed to specific channels instead of
processing the full stereo mix. Channel-selected effects are applied
pre-mix via sub-mix buses (channel → subMix → effect → masterInput),
giving proper serial/inline processing.

New features:
- selectedChannels field on EffectConfig type
- Channel checkbox row on each master FX pedal (collapsible)
- ChannelRoutedEffectsManager handles sub-mix routing
- ToneEngine splits effects into channel-routed vs global during rebuild
- masterEffectsKey includes selectedChannels for rebuild triggers
- Send bus presets auto-route all channels at 50% send level

Architecture: channel-selected effects run BEFORE the mix point,
global master effects run AFTER (post-mix chain unchanged).
